steps.arg: If you want to see more examples , see  List Of RSCG
My name is Andrei Ignat and I am deeply fond of Roslyn Source Code Generator.

Today I will present SvgIconGenerator .  Generates strongly-typed C# classes from SVG icon files at compile time — access SVG icons as typed properties with no runtime file I/O.How to use1. Add SVG files as AdditionalFiles in .csproj:```xml  <ItemGroup>      <AdditionalFiles Include="Icons/*.svg" />  </ItemGroup>```  3. Declare a partial static class with [GenerateIcons]:```charp  [GenerateIcons()]  internal static partial class MyIcons;```  3. Access icons as typed members (one per SVG file):```charp  Console.WriteLine(MyIcons.Circle.Name);        // "Circle"  Console.WriteLine(MyIcons.Rect.InnerContent);  // SVG inner XML``` You can use for  Embedding SVG icons as compile-time constants with typed access — no file paths, no reflection, no runtime loading. .
You can download the code from here
So , let's start the project with Visual Studio Code
To use it ,you will put the Nuget SvgIconGenerator into the csproj
And now I will show you an example of using SvgIconGenerator
now execute the tour in VSCode
And I will execute the project
